description: 26F twisted ankle playing soccer, can't bear weight — Ottawa Ankle candidate
transcript:
Dr. Wu: What happened?
Patient: I was playing soccer this afternoon. I went to plant my foot and rolled my ankle inward. I felt a pop and went down.
Dr. Wu: Were you able to walk after?
Patient: No. My friends had to help me off the field. I tried to put weight on it once and it was way too painful.
Dr. Wu: Have you been able to walk at all since?
Patient: Maybe a couple of steps in the parking lot, hopping mostly. Not really walking.
Dr. Wu: How old are you?
Patient: Twenty-six.
Dr. Wu: Any other injuries? Knee, hip, back?
Patient: No, just the ankle.
Dr. Wu: Any medical problems? Pregnancy?
Patient: No.
Dr. Wu: Let me take a look. Significant swelling around the lateral malleolus. Tender right at the posterior edge of the lateral malleolus, also along the distal six centimeters of the fibula. Medial side is non-tender. Foot midfoot non-tender. Navicular non-tender. Base of fifth metatarsal non-tender.
Dr. Wu: Can you put any weight on it now?
Patient: I really can't.
Dr. Wu: Thinking about whether she needs an X-ray.

groundTruth.notes: Classic Ottawa Ankle case. Ankle injury with malleolar pain, posterior lateral malleolus tenderness, inability to weight-bear immediately and in ED. Doctor verbally enters the decision. Surface with the heard criteria pre-filled.
